commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5e4ffece204dc5f855a2429e2088d3399a5c8878)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WinUI Workspace session file rail using typed `sessions.files.list/get` APIs, localized UI strings, a projection model, and focused tray tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows current main still uses `agents.files.list/get`, while this PR loads through `sessions.files.list/get` and shows unsupported when that typed method is unavailable. I did not run a live old-gateway setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the compatibility policy for replacing `agents.files` with `sessions.files`; automation should not guess whether to preserve fallback or require coordinated gateway upgrades.

target.title: Add Workspace session file rail
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: The Windows Workspace page did not expose a session-scoped file rail/browser for files the active agent read or edited. - Why it matters: Operators need a quick way to inspect the files involved in a session without treating the Workspace page as an unrestricted file explorer. - What changed: Added a typed `sessions.files.list` / `sessions.files.get`-backed file rail with Edited/Read/Missing metadata, folder browsing, server-side search, truncation messaging, right-click copy path, safe previews, and Markdown rendered/raw viewing. - User impact: Companion Settings → Workspace now shows the active session's file context and lets users preview session-relevant files safely. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Arbitrary browser-only file preview and artifact rows are not added here; browser-only rows are visible for navigation but not previewed unless the session read or edited them. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs / instructions - [x] Tests / validation - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore / infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Tray / WinUI UX - [ ] Windows node capability - [ ] Local MCP / `winnode` - [x] Gateway / connection / pairing - [ ] Setup / onboarding - [x] Permissions / privacy / security - [x] Tests / CI / docs ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #871 - [ ] Related to a bug or regression ## Validation - `./build.ps1` — passed - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — 2567 passed, 31 skipped, 2598 total - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — 1323 passed, 0 skipped, 1323 total ## Real behavior proof - Environment tested: Windows ARM64, Debug build, non-isolated tray launch from this branch - PR head / commit tested: `5e4ffece` - Exact steps or command run: Opened OpenClaw tray app, navigated to Companion Settings → Workspace, exercised the session file rail and required validation commands above. - Evidence after fix: Workspace page shows the file rail/browser with session file metadata, search/path browsing, preview pane, and row context menu copy path. - Observed result: Session-relevant files can be previewed safely; browser-only files remain visible for navigation but are not previewed unless the session read or edited them. - Screenshot/artifact links verified? (`Yes/No/N/A`): Yes - Not verified / blocked: Artifact rows remain follow-up work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): Yes - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The UI now consumes existing typed session file list/get APIs. Access remains session-scoped, browser-only files are not previewed unless the session read or edited them, and unavailable/too-large/missing content renders as an inline unavailable state instead of dumping content. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.
target.author: bkudiess
